channel.title: Ad News Feed

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Ads with $20/Day: Budget Tutorial Revealed #shorts
item.description: Can you really run Google Ads with just $20 per day? We break down when it's possible and when it's impossible to make a small budget work with Google Ads. #GoogleAds #SmallBudget #PPC #DigitalMarketing

item.title: [General] Paid Ads: Automate Your Practice for Consistent Patient Flow #shorts
item.description: Turn your practice into an autopilot machine with paid advertising. Ensure a consistent customer stream, even with competition. Drive more patients to your door, every single day. #PaidAdvertising #PracticeGrowth #CustomerAcquisition #MarketingStrategy

item.title: [General] Emergency Service SEO: How to Capture “Near Me,” “Open Now,” and 24/7 Searches That Turn Into Calls
item.description: When someone searches for an emergency plumber, an after-hours electrician, a 24/7 locksmith, or a same-day HVAC repair company, they are not browsing. They are trying to solve a problem that is happening right now. A pipe is leaking. The AC is out in peak heat. A storefront door will not lock. A

item.title: [General] Neighborhood SEO for Real Estate: 15 Proven Ways to Win High-Intent Local Search Terms
item.description: Real estate search rarely starts with an agent’s name. It starts with place. A buyer looks up “best neighborhoods in Frisco for families.” A relocating seller searches “homes near downtown Naperville.” An investor types “cash flow rental neighborhoods in Tampa.” Even when the final conversion hap

item.title: [General] Law Firm SEO: The 90-Day Roadmap to Page One for Local, Practice Area, and AI Search Visibility
item.description: Law firm SEO is difficult for a simple reason: legal search is one of the most competitive, trust-sensitive environments on the internet. Prospective clients are not looking for entertainment. They are looking for answers that affect money, freedom, family, immigration status, business risk, or p

item.title: [General] How to Rank for “Near Me” Medical Searches in Competitive Markets: A Practical Local SEO Guide for Clinics, Groups, and Hospitals
item.description: When a patient searches for “urgent care near me,” “dermatologist near me,” “OBGYN near me,” or “walk-in clinic near me,” they are usually not browsing casually. They are trying to solve a real problem close to where they are, often on a phone, often with limited patience, and often with the inte

item.title: [General] E-commerce Schema Markup for Rich Results: Product, Merchant Listing, Variant, and Category Page Implementation Guide
item.description: Search engines do not buy products. People do. But search engines decide which product pages, category pages, store policies, and brand signals get understood well enough to be shown in enhanced search experiences. That is where e-commerce schema markup matters. For online stores, structured data

item.title: [General] 12 Best Marketing Conferences in May 2026: Dates, Locations, Costs, and Who Should Attend
item.description: May 2026 is one of the strongest months on the calendar for marketing conferences. It sits at an unusually useful point in the year: Q1 planning is over, budgets are more defined, and teams are far enough from January strategy sessions to know where they need help. That is why May tends to attrac

item.title: [General] SEO ROI: How to Calculate, Forecast, and Improve Organic Search Returns
item.description: Search engine optimization is one of the few marketing investments that can keep producing value long after the original work is done. A well-built page can attract qualified visitors for months or years. A technically sound site can convert more of the traffic you already have. A strong organic

item.title: [General] Lexus x Laufey Blue In Green Edition RZ: 17 Facts About the 100 Miles Miles Davis Campaign
item.description: When Lexus unveiled the Blue In Green Edition RZ with Laufey as part of its “100 Miles” campaign, the announcement did more than introduce a one-off custom electric vehicle. It connected a luxury automaker, a contemporary artist with deep jazz influence, one of the most important names in modern

item.title: [General] Apple 50th Anniversary Campaign Explained: What “50 Years of Thinking Different” Reveals About Brand Longevity
item.description: Apple turning 50 was never going to be treated as a routine corporate anniversary. A company founded on April 1, 1976, and built through a series of category-defining products had a rare opportunity in 2026: mark a historic milestone without looking backward for its own sake. What Apple did inste

item.title: [General] KitKat Heist: How Nestlé Responded After 413,793 Chocolate Bars Went Missing
item.description: A truck carrying 413,793 KitKat bars vanished while traveling from central Italy to Poland. The shipment weighed roughly 12 metric tons, consisted of Formula 1-themed KitKat products, and disappeared at a time of year when confectionery demand is especially sensitive. On paper, the story looked l

item.title: [General] Customer Lifecycle Strategy, Attribution Updates, and More

item.description: A traffic decline used to trigger a fairly predictable conversation. Check rankings. Review the last site changes. Look for an algorithm update. Fix the obvious issues. Wait for recovery. That playbook is still necessary, but it is no longer sufficient. Today, a drop in organic traffic can mean v

item.title: [General] PUMA 2026 Kit Launch in NYC: 11 National Team Jerseys, Brooklyn Event Highlights, Release Date, and What It Means
item.description: PUMA did not introduce its 2026 national team kits through a standard press conference, a stadium reveal, or a film-first campaign. Instead, it brought the launch into Brooklyn, where football, music, food, design, and community programming became the format for the unveiling itself. That choice

item.title: [General] Mr. Clean’s Biggest Magic Eraser Upgrade in 20 Years: What Changed, How It Means for Home Cleaning, and Which New Product Fits the Job
item.description: For a long time, the Mr. Clean Magic Eraser occupied a familiar place in American homes: part sponge, part problem-solver, something people reached for when a spray cleaner stalled out on scuffs, soap scum, or kitchen grime. That baseline familiarity is exactly why the brand’s latest product upda

item.title: [General] Coca-Cola’s “You Must Love Coke” Optical Illusion Campaign Explained: Why Red Appears in Blue Football Stadiums
item.description: Coca-Cola’s “You Must Love Coke” campaign is one of those rare brand activations that becomes more interesting the longer you look at it. On the surface, the premise is simple: place Coca-Cola in football environments where its signature red is unwelcome, and do it without actually using red. But

item.title: [General] Meta’s Adaptive Ranking Model: What Instagram’s Ad Serving Update Means for CTR, Conversions, and ROAS
item.description: Meta has outlined a new improvement to its ad serving program, centered on what it calls the Adaptive Ranking Model. On the surface, that sounds like another technical infrastructure update. In practice, it matters because ad serving is the mechanism that decides which ad gets shown to which pers

item.title: [General] Meta’s New Ray-Ban Prescription Smart Glasses: Price, Features, Privacy Questions, and What Buyers Need to Know
item.description: Meta has taken a practical next step in smart eyewear by introducing new Ray-Ban Meta glasses designed to better support prescription lenses. That may sound like a small product update, but it addresses one of the biggest barriers keeping smart glasses from becoming a genuine everyday device. A c

item.title: [General] Liquid Death and Taylor Morrison House Giveaway Explained: How the Soda House Works, How to Enter, Prize Details, and Why the Campaign Matters
item.description: If a headline about a home with Liquid Death flowing from the faucets made you stop and read twice, that reaction is part of the point. The new Liquid Death and Taylor Morrison giveaway is built to do exactly that: interrupt attention, create conversation, and turn a sweepstakes into a piece of c

item.title: [General] Best April Fools’ Day Ads of 2026: 23 Brand Campaigns That Worked and What Marketers Should Learn
item.description: April Fools’ Day marketing is easy to dismiss as seasonal internet noise. Every year, brands invent fake products, post a polished visual, and wait to see whether audiences laugh, share, or roll their eyes. Yet the best April Fools’ Day ads of 2026 show that these campaigns are not really about r

item.title: [General] Mike’s Hard Lemonade Animated Lemons Campaign: How “Made with Real Character” Targets Gen Z and Launches Mike’s Dirty Lemonade
item.description: If you want to understand where beverage marketing is heading in 2026, Mike’s Hard Lemonade offers a useful case study. The brand did not simply launch a new flavor line. It introduced a new creative platform, new recurring characters, a new product tied to a social-media-driven drink trend, and

item.title: [General] Coca-Cola’s New “And a Coke” Campaign Explained: Why Coke Still Leads Pepsi in Fast Food and What It Means for Restaurant Marketing
item.description: Coca-Cola has returned to one of its strongest competitive positions in U.S. foodservice with a campaign that does something unusually direct for a brand of its size: it reminds consumers that, across much of fast food, ordering a meal and ordering a Coke are still deeply linked. The new “And a C

item.title: [Meta Ads] Does a Higher CPM Mean You Should Spend More?
item.description: Today's question is about CPM being double in the US versus the UK and whether that means you should allocate double the budget. item.title: [General] Marketing KPIs: 25 Metrics to Track Leads, Revenue, and ROI
item.description: Marketing becomes much easier to manage when everyone agrees on what success actually looks like. That sounds obvious, but in practice, many teams still report on activity instead of outcomes. They celebrate more traffic, more impressions, more followers, or more email sends without proving wheth

item.title: [General] AI Visibility: How to Increase Brand Mentions and Citations in Google AI Overviews, ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Claude
item.description: AI visibility is the ability of your brand, website, and content to appear in AI-generated answers when people ask questions in tools like Google AI Overviews, ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, and other answer engines. It is no longer enough to rank for a keyword and hope a user clicks through ten bl

item.title: [General] SEO Techniques That Still Matter in 2026: 17 Proven Ways to Improve Rankings, Traffic, and AI Search Visibility
item.description: Search engine optimization is still one of the most dependable ways to grow qualified traffic, improve visibility, and turn demand into revenue. item.title: [Google Ads] Google Analytics Dashboard Guide: How to Build a GA4 Dashboard for Traffic, Conversions, Revenue, and SEO
item.description: A good Google Analytics dashboard does one job better than almost anything else in marketing: it turns too much data into a smaller set of decisions. That sounds simple, but in practice it is where most teams get stuck. Google Analytics 4 gives you a large volume of event-based data, multiple rep

item.title: [Google Ads] Get More Leads with Google Ads [2026 Step by Step Tutorial]
item.description:
In 2026, getting leads on Google is easy... but getting profitable leads? That's a different story.
Most service businesses are paying a laziness tax, letting Google's AI chase cheap, junk clicks that never convert. In this tutorial, I'm breaking down exactly how to take back control of your Google

item.title: [General] Boost E-commerce SEO: Unlock Driving Traffic Now! #shorts
item.description: Turn search queries into compelling content pages. Learn how to create ultimate guides and informative pages that target keywords like 'most forgiving driver' to drive SEO traffic to your e-commerce site. #EcommerceSEO #ContentMarketing #KeywordResearch #DigitalStrategy #SEOTips

item.title: [Meta Ads] What Meta Isn't Telling You About Your Creative
item.description: Meta provides no transparency about which specific images or videos perform best when using flexible format, related media, or AI generated creative. Breakdowns exist but share almost no useful detail. Jon explains why this lack of transparency is intentional at this point, why it matters for creati

item.title: [Meta Ads] Meta広告で結局どの画像サイズを用意すればいい？押さえるべきアスペクト比の考え方
item.description: Meta広告のクリエイティブを準備するとき、「結局どのサイズを用意すればいいの？」という疑問にぶつかる方は多いのではないでしょうか。配信面ごとに推奨サイズが異なるうえ、公式ヘルプの情報も分散していて全…

item.title: [General] Audience Segments Change Everything

That is why white label citation building exists. A

item.title: [General] White Label Shopify SEO: 21 Product Page Optimization Tactics That Improve Traffic and Conversions
item.description: Shopify product pages sit at the point where search visibility, merchandising, user experience, and revenue all meet. For agencies offering white label Shopify SEO, that makes the product page the most important place to get right. A category page may bring in broader discovery traffic. A blog ar

item.title: [Google Ads] E-commerce PPC Management: The Complete Guide to White Label Google Shopping Campaigns for Agencies and Online Stores
item.description: Google Shopping is one of the most commercially direct channels in e-commerce PPC. A user searches for a product, sees an image, price, merchant name, reviews or promotions, and clicks with a clearer idea of what they are about to buy. That is why Shopping traffic often behaves differently from s

item.title: [General] Core Web Vitals Optimization for Agencies: White Label Site Speed Fixes That Improve LCP, INP, CLS, SEO, and Conversions
item.description: If you run an agency, site speed is rarely just a technical issue. It becomes an account management issue, a retention issue, a margin issue, and eventually a growth issue. Clients do not usually call and say, “Our Interaction to Next Paint is too high.” They say their traffic has stalled, leads

item.title: [General] White Label Link Building Services: What Agencies Should Buy, What to Avoid, and How Quality Links Move Rankings
item.description: Link building is still one of the hardest SEO services for agencies to deliver well at scale. It is slow, relationship-driven, quality-sensitive, and easy to get wrong. Most agencies can sell SEO. Far fewer can consistently earn relevant, editorial backlinks that improve search visibility without

item.title: [General] Schema Markup Implementation: The Complete Guide to White Label Structured Data Services for Agencies
item.description: Search engines do not read websites the way people do. A human visitor can scan a page, recognize that it is a service page, notice the business name, identify the offer, and understand whether the company serves a local market or a national one. Search engines need more explicit signals. That is

item.title: [General] Amazon Marketing Services for Sellers: How White Label PPC + SEO Improve Visibility, Efficiency, and Sales
item.description: If you work with Amazon sellers, you have probably noticed that the phrase Amazon Marketing Services still shows up everywhere, even though Amazon’s ad ecosystem has evolved well beyond the original label. Sellers, agencies, and brand teams still use the term as shorthand for the work that actual

item.title: [General] Outreach Link Building for Agencies: The White Label Solutions Teams Are Switching To for Better Links, Safer Delivery, and Stronger Client Retention
item.description: When agencies talk about link building, they often use one phrase to describe several very different services. One vendor sells placements from a fixed website inventory. Another pitches digital PR. Another offers niche edits. Another runs outreach but outsources content. Another buys media place

item.title: [General] How to Resell Local SEO: The Complete Guide to Pricing, Fulfillment, and Client Retention for Marketing Agencies
item.description: If you run a marketing agency, sooner or later a client asks a version of the same question: can you help us show up higher in Google Maps, local search, and “near me” results? For many agencies, that moment creates a choice. You can say no and leave revenue on the table. You can try […]

item.title: [General] Orkin SEO Strategy: Keyword Research for Pest Control #shorts
item.description: Learn how to strategically pull keywords for a service-based business like Orkin. We explore targeting options for pest control, termite control, and more within home services. #KeywordResearch #PestControl #DigitalMarketing #SEOtips #Orkin

item.title: [Meta Ads] Audience Segments Change Everything
item.description: When you use audience segments, they will change your understanding of algorithmic targeting and remarketing. Learn how to create them, break down results, and apply what you learn in my free mini-course. Subscribe at jonloomer.com/segments-subscribe.

item.title: [Google Ads] New Google Ad Manager API read-only scope
item.description:
The Ad Manager API now supports a read-only OAuth scope. This new scope, https://www.googleapis.com/auth/admanager.readonly, allows developers to request read-only access to their users' Ad Manager data.

This new scope is only available for the

item.title: [Google Ads] Increase in Batch Job Service Request Size
item.description:

item.title: [Microsoft Ads] Debunking the myth: Is AI-powered search killing ad success?
item.description: AI isn’t replacing search - it’s reshaping it. Learn how Search First brands stay visible and influential across AI driven, zero click customer journeys.

item.title: [Google Ads] Master Google Ads: Keyword Planner Secrets Revealed! #shorts
item.description: Discover how to leverage Google Ads Keyword Planner for long-tail variations. Filter by monthly searches and top-of-page bids to find your most valuable keywords for SEO success. #KeywordPlanner #GoogleAds #SEOStrategy #DigitalMarketing
